member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that an API is experimental and it may change in the future.
member.remarks: This attribute allows call sites to be flagged with a diagnostic that indicates that an experimental feature is used. Authors can use this attribute to ship preview features in their assemblies.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, specifying the ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.
member.param: The ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.DiagnosticId
member.summary: Gets the ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.
member.remarks:
The diagnostic ID is shown in build output for warnings and errors.
This property represents the unique ID that can be used to suppress the warnings or errors, if needed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.UrlFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the URL for corresponding documentation. The API accepts a format string instead of an actual URL, creating a generic URL that includes the diagnostic ID.
member.value: The format string that represents a URL to corresponding documentation.
member.remarks: An example format string is https://contoso.com/obsoletion-warnings/{0}.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified method requires the ability to generate new code at runtime, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when compiling ahead of time.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires dynamic code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified method requires dynamic access to code that is not referenced statically, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when removing unreferenced code from an application.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of unreferenced code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of unreferenced code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires unreferenced code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute
member.summary: Suppresses reporting of a specific rule violation, allowing multiple suppressions on a single code artifact.
member.remarks: is different than in that it doesn't have a . So it is always preserved in the compiled assembly.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, specifying the category of the tool and the identifier for an analysis rule.
member.param: The category for the attribute.
The identifier of the analysis rule the attribute applies to.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Category
member.summary: Gets the category identifying the classification of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property describes the tool or tool analysis category for which a message suppression attribute applies.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.CheckId
member.summary: Gets the identifier of the analysis tool rule to be suppressed.
member.remarks: Concatenated together, the and properties form a unique check identifier.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Scope
member.summary: Gets or sets the scope of the code that is relevant for the attribute.
member.remarks: The Scope property is an optional argument that specifies the metadata scope for which the attribute is relevant.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Target
member.summary: Gets or sets a fully qualified path that represents the target of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument identifying the analysis target of the attribute. An example value is "System.IO.Stream.ctor():System.Void". Because it is fully qualified, it can be long, particularly for targets such as parameters. The analysis tool user interface should be capable of automatically formatting the parameter.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.MessageId
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional argument expanding on exclusion criteria.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument that specifies additional exclusion where the literal metadata target is not sufficiently precise. For example, the cannot be applied within a method, and it may be desirable to suppress a violation against a statement in the method that will give a rule violation, but not against all statements in the method.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Justification
member.summary: Gets or sets the justification for suppressing the code analysis message.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute
member.summary: States a dependency that one member has on another.
member.remarks: This can be used to inform tooling of a dependency that is otherwise not evident purely from metadata and IL, for example a member relied on via reflection.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified signature of a member on the same type as the consumer.
member.param: The signature of the member depended on.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.Type)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified signature of a member on a .
member.param: The signature of the member depended on.
The containing .

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified signature of a member on a type in an assembly.
member.param: The signature of the member depended on.
The full name of the type containing the specified member.
The assembly name of the type containing the specified member.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes,System.Type)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified types of members on a .
member.param: The types of members depended on.
The containing the specified members.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes,System.String,System.String)

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.MemberSignature
member.summary: Gets the signature of the member depended on.
member.remarks: Either must be a valid string or must not equal , but not both.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.MemberTypes
member.summary: Gets the which specifies the type of members depended on.
member.remarks: Either must be a valid string or must not equal , but not both.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.Type
member.summary: Gets the containing the specified member.
member.remarks: If neither nor are specified, the type of the consumer is assumed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.TypeName
member.summary: Gets the full name of the type containing the specified member.
member.remarks: If neither nor are specified, the type of the consumer is assumed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.AssemblyName
member.summary: Gets the assembly name of the specified type.
member.remarks: is only valid when is specified.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.Condition
member.summary: Gets or sets the condition in which the dependency is applicable, e.g. "DEBUG".

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that certain members on a specified are accessed dynamically, for example through .
member.remarks:
This allows tools to understand which members are being accessed during the execution of a program.
This attribute is valid on members whose type is or .
When this attribute is applied to a location of type , the assumption is that the string represents a fully qualified type name.
When this attribute is applied to a class, interface, or struct, the members specified can be accessed dynamically on instances returned from calling on instances of that class, interface, or struct.
If the attribute is applied to a method it's treated as a special case and it implies the attribute should be applied to the "this" parameter of the method. As such the attribute should only be used on instance methods of types assignable to System.Type (or string, but no methods will use it there).

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute.#ctor(System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified member types.
member.param: The types of members dynamically accessed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute.MemberTypes
member.summary: Gets the which specifies the type of members dynamically accessed.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes
member.summary:
Specifies the types of members that are dynamically accessed.
This enumeration has a attribute that allows a bitwise combination of its member values.

member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.FileShare
member.summary: FileShare.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.FileShare.Name
member.summary: The name of the file share within the specified storage account. File share names must be between 3 and 63 characters in length and use numbers, lower-case letters and dash (-) only. Every dash (-) character must be immediately preceded and followed by a letter or number.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.FileShare.AccessTier
member.summary: Access tier for specific share. GpV2 account can choose between TransactionOptimized (default), Hot, and Cool. FileStorage account can choose Premium.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.FileShare.EnabledProtocol
member.summary: The authentication protocol that is used for the file share. Can only be specified when creating a share.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.FileShare.FileSharePaidBursting
member.summary: File Share Paid Bursting properties.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.FileShare.Metadata
member.summary: A name-value pair to associate with the share as metadata.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.FileShare.ProvisionedBandwidthMibps
member.summary: The provisioned bandwidth of the share, in mebibytes per second. This property is only for file shares created under Files Provisioned v2 account type. Please refer to the GetFileServiceUsage API response for the minimum and maximum allowed value for provisioned bandwidth.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.FileShare.ProvisionedIops
member.summary: The provisioned IOPS of the share. This property is only for file shares created under Files Provisioned v2 account type. Please refer to the GetFileServiceUsage API response for the minimum and maximum allowed value for provisioned IOPS.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.FileShare.RootSquash
member.summary: The property is for NFS share only. The default is NoRootSquash.

For file shares created under Files Provisioned v2 account type, please refer to the GetFileServiceUsage API response for the minimum and maximum allowed provisioned storage size.

member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.ImmutabilityPolicy
member.summary: ImmutabilityPolicy.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.ImmutabilityPolicy.Name
member.summary: Gets the Name.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.ImmutabilityPolicy.AllowProtectedAppendWrites
member.summary: This property can only be changed for unlocked time-based retention policies. When enabled, new blocks can be written to an append blob while maintaining immutability protection and compliance. Only new blocks can be added and any existing blocks cannot be modified or deleted. This property cannot be changed with ExtendImmutabilityPolicy API.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.ImmutabilityPolicy.AllowProtectedAppendWritesAll
member.summary: This property can only be changed for unlocked time-based retention policies. When enabled, new blocks can be written to both 'Append and Bock Blobs' while maintaining immutability protection and compliance. Only new blocks can be added and any existing blocks cannot be modified or deleted. This property cannot be changed with ExtendImmutabilityPolicy API. The 'allowProtectedAppendWrites' and 'allowProtectedAppendWritesAll' properties are mutually exclusive.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.ImmutabilityPolicy.ImmutabilityPeriodSinceCreationInDays
member.summary: The immutability period for the blobs in the container since the policy creation, in days.

member.summary: Gets the SystemData.

member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.AccountImmutabilityPolicy
member.summary: This defines account-level immutability policy properties.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.AccountImmutabilityPolicy.ImmutabilityPeriodSinceCreationInDays
member.summary: The immutability period for the blobs in the container since the policy creation, in days.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.AccountImmutabilityPolicy.State
member.summary: The ImmutabilityPolicy state defines the mode of the policy. Disabled state disables the policy, Unlocked state allows increase and decrease of immutability retention time and also allows toggling allowProtectedAppendWrites property, Locked state only allows the increase of the immutability retention time. A policy can only be created in a Disabled or Unlocked state and can be toggled between the two states. Only a policy in an Unlocked state can transition to a Locked state which cannot be reverted.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.AccountImmutabilityPolicy.AllowProtectedAppendWrites
member.summary: This property can only be changed for disabled and unlocked time-based retention policies. When enabled, new blocks can be written to an append blob while maintaining immutability protection and compliance. Only new blocks can be added and any existing blocks cannot be modified or deleted.

member.name: M:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.AccountImmutabilityPolicy.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a new AccountImmutabilityPolicy.

member.summary: Define all the provisionable properties of AccountImmutabilityPolicy.

member.summary: The ImmutabilityPolicy state defines the mode of the policy. Disabled state disables the policy, Unlocked state allows increase and decrease of immutability retention time and also allows toggling allowProtectedAppendWrites property, Locked state only allows the increase of the immutability retention time. A policy can only be created in a Disabled or Unlocked state and can be toggled between the two states. Only a policy in an Unlocked state can transition to a Locked state which cannot be reverted.

member.summary: Unlocked.

member.summary: Locked.

member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.BlobInventoryPolicyDefinition
member.summary: An object that defines the blob inventory rule.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.BlobInventoryPolicyDefinition.Filters
member.summary: An object that defines the filter set.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.BlobInventoryPolicyDefinition.Format
member.summary: This is a required field, it specifies the format for the inventory files.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.BlobInventoryPolicyDefinition.Schedule
member.summary: This is a required field. This field is used to schedule an inventory formation.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.BlobInventoryPolicyDefinition.ObjectType
member.summary: This is a required field. This field specifies the scope of the inventory created either at the blob or container level.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.BlobInventoryPolicyDefinition.SchemaFields
member.summary: This is a required field. This field specifies the fields and properties of the object to be included in the inventory. The Schema field value 'Name' is always required. The valid values for this field for the 'Blob' definition.objectType include 'Name, Creation-Time, Last-Modified, Content-Length, Content-MD5, BlobType, AccessTier, AccessTierChangeTime, AccessTierInferred, Tags, Expiry-Time, hdi_isfolder, Owner, Group, Permissions, Acl, Snapshot, VersionId, IsCurrentVersion, Metadata, LastAccessTime, Tags, Etag, ContentType, ContentEncoding, ContentLanguage, ContentCRC64, CacheControl, ContentDisposition, LeaseStatus, LeaseState, LeaseDuration, ServerEncrypted, Deleted, DeletionId, DeletedTime, RemainingRetentionDays, ImmutabilityPolicyUntilDate, ImmutabilityPolicyMode, LegalHold, CopyId, CopyStatus, CopySource, CopyProgress, CopyCompletionTime, CopyStatusDescription, CustomerProvidedKeySha256, RehydratePriority, ArchiveStatus, XmsBlobSequenceNumber, EncryptionScope, IncrementalCopy, TagCount'. For Blob object type schema field value 'DeletedTime' is applicable only for Hns enabled accounts. The valid values for 'Container' definition.objectType include 'Name, Last-Modified, Metadata, LeaseStatus, LeaseState, LeaseDuration, PublicAccess, HasImmutabilityPolicy, HasLegalHold, Etag, DefaultEncryptionScope, DenyEncryptionScopeOverride, ImmutableStorageWithVersioningEnabled, Deleted, Version, DeletedTime, RemainingRetentionDays'. Schema field values 'Expiry-Time, hdi_isfolder, Owner, Group, Permissions, Acl, DeletionId' are valid only for Hns enabled accounts.Schema field values 'Tags, TagCount' are only valid for Non-Hns accounts.

member.name: M:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.BlobInventoryPolicyDefinition.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a new BlobInventoryPolicyDefinition.

member.name: M:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.BlobInventoryPolicyDefinition.DefineProvisionableProperties
member.summary: Define all the provisionable properties of BlobInventoryPolicyDefinition.

member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.BlobInventoryPolicyFilter
member.summary: An object that defines the blob inventory rule filter conditions. For 'Blob' definition.objectType all filter properties are applicable, 'blobTypes' is required and others are optional. For 'Container' definition.objectType only prefixMatch is applicable and is optional.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.BlobInventoryPolicyFilter.IncludePrefix
member.summary: An array of strings with maximum 10 blob prefixes to be included in the inventory.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.BlobInventoryPolicyFilter.ExcludePrefix
member.summary: An array of strings with maximum 10 blob prefixes to be excluded from the inventory.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.BlobInventoryPolicyFilter.BlobTypes
member.summary: An array of predefined enum values. Valid values include blockBlob, appendBlob, pageBlob. Hns accounts does not support pageBlobs. This field is required when definition.objectType property is set to 'Blob'.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.BlobInventoryPolicyFilter.IncludeBlobVersions
member.summary: Includes blob versions in blob inventory when value is set to true. The definition.schemaFields values 'VersionId and IsCurrentVersion' are required if this property is set to true, else they must be excluded.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.BlobInventoryPolicyFilter.IncludeSnapshots
member.summary: Includes blob snapshots in blob inventory when value is set to true. The definition.schemaFields value 'Snapshot' is required if this property is set to true, else it must be excluded.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.BlobInventoryPolicyFilter.IncludeDeleted
member.summary: For 'Container' definition.objectType the definition.schemaFields must include 'Deleted, Version, DeletedTime and RemainingRetentionDays'. For 'Blob' definition.objectType and HNS enabled storage accounts the definition.schemaFields must include 'DeletionId, Deleted, DeletedTime and RemainingRetentionDays' and for Hns disabled accounts the definition.schemaFields must include 'Deleted and RemainingRetentionDays', else it must be excluded.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.BlobInventoryPolicyFilter.CreationTimeLastNDays
member.summary: When set the policy filters the objects that are created in the last N days. Where N is an integer value between 1 to 36500.

member.summary: Creates a new BlobInventoryPolicyFilter.

member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.DateAfterCreation
member.summary: Object to define snapshot and version action conditions.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.DateAfterCreation.DaysAfterCreationGreaterThan
member.summary: Value indicating the age in days after creation.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.DateAfterCreation.DaysAfterLastTierChangeGreaterThan
member.summary: Value indicating the age in days after last blob tier change time. This property is only applicable for tierToArchive actions and requires daysAfterCreationGreaterThan to be set for snapshots and blob version based actions. The blob will be archived if both the conditions are satisfied.

member.name: M:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.DateAfterCreation.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a new DateAfterCreation.

member.name: M:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.DateAfterCreation.DefineProvisionableProperties
member.summary: Define all the provisionable properties of DateAfterCreation.

member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.DateAfterModification
member.summary: Object to define the base blob action conditions. Properties daysAfterModificationGreaterThan, daysAfterLastAccessTimeGreaterThan and daysAfterCreationGreaterThan are mutually exclusive. The daysAfterLastTierChangeGreaterThan property is only applicable for tierToArchive actions which requires daysAfterModificationGreaterThan to be set, also it cannot be used in conjunction with daysAfterLastAccessTimeGreaterThan or daysAfterCreationGreaterThan.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.DateAfterModification.DaysAfterModificationGreaterThan
member.summary: Value indicating the age in days after last modification.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.DateAfterModification.DaysAfterLastAccessTimeGreaterThan
member.summary: Value indicating the age in days after last blob access. This property can only be used in conjunction with last access time tracking policy.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.DateAfterModification.DaysAfterLastTierChangeGreaterThan
member.summary: Value indicating the age in days after last blob tier change time. This property is only applicable for tierToArchive actions and requires daysAfterModificationGreaterThan to be set for baseBlobs based actions. The blob will be archived if both the conditions are satisfied.

member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.GeoReplicationStatistics
member.summary: Statistics related to replication for storage account's Blob, Table, Queue and File services. It is only available when geo-redundant replication is enabled for the storage account.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.GeoReplicationStatistics.Status
member.summary: The status of the secondary location. Possible values are: - Live: Indicates that the secondary location is active and operational. - Bootstrap: Indicates initial synchronization from the primary location to the secondary location is in progress.This typically occurs when replication is first enabled. - Unavailable: Indicates that the secondary location is temporarily unavailable.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.GeoReplicationStatistics.LastSyncOn
member.summary: All primary writes preceding this UTC date/time value are guaranteed to be available for read operations. Primary writes following this point in time may or may not be available for reads. Element may be default value if value of LastSyncTime is not available, this can happen if secondary is offline or we are in bootstrap.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.GeoReplicationStatistics.CanFailover
member.summary: A boolean flag which indicates whether or not account failover is supported for the account.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.GeoReplicationStatistics.CanPlannedFailover
member.summary: A boolean flag which indicates whether or not planned account failover is supported for the account.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.GeoReplicationStatistics.PostFailoverRedundancy
member.summary: The redundancy type of the account after an account failover is performed.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.GeoReplicationStatistics.PostPlannedFailoverRedundancy
member.summary: The redundancy type of the account after a planned account failover is performed.

member.name: M:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.GeoReplicationStatistics.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a new GeoReplicationStatistics.

member.summary: Define all the provisionable properties of GeoReplicationStatistics.

member.summary: The status of the secondary location. Possible values are: - Live: Indicates that the secondary location is active and operational. - Bootstrap: Indicates initial synchronization from the primary location to the secondary location is in progress.This typically occurs when replication is first enabled. - Unavailable: Indicates that the secondary location is temporarily unavailable.

member.summary: Live.

member.summary: Bootstrap.

member.summary: This property enables and defines account-level immutability. Enabling the feature auto-enables Blob Versioning.

member.summary: A boolean flag which enables account-level immutability. All the containers under such an account have object-level immutability enabled by default.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.ImmutableStorageAccount.ImmutabilityPolicy
member.summary: Specifies the default account-level immutability policy which is inherited and applied to objects that do not possess an explicit immutability policy at the object level. The object-level immutability policy has higher precedence than the container-level immutability policy, which has a higher precedence than the account-level immutability policy.

member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.ObjectReplicationPolicyFilter
member.summary: Filters limit replication to a subset of blobs within the storage account. A logical OR is performed on values in the filter. If multiple filters are defined, a logical AND is performed on all filters.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.ObjectReplicationPolicyFilter.PrefixMatch
member.summary: Optional. Filters the results to replicate only blobs whose names begin with the specified prefix.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.ObjectReplicationPolicyFilter.MinCreationTime
member.summary: Blobs created after the time will be replicated to the destination. It must be in datetime format 'yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ'. Example: 2020-02-19T16:05:00Z.

member.summary: Kerberos ticket encryption supported by server. Valid values are RC4-HMAC, AES-256. Should be passed as a string with delimiter ';'.

member.summary: SMB channel encryption supported by server. Valid values are AES-128-CCM, AES-128-GCM, AES-256-GCM. Should be passed as a string with delimiter ';'.

member.summary: Start time of the run instance. Filter options such as startTime gt '2023-06-26T20:51:24.4494016Z' and other comparison operators can be used as described for DateTime properties in https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/storageservices/querying-tables-and-entities#supported-comparison-operators.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageTaskReportProperties.FinishedOn
member.summary: End time of the run instance. Filter options such as startTime gt '2023-06-26T20:51:24.4494016Z' and other comparison operators can be used as described for DateTime properties in https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/storageservices/querying-tables-and-entities#supported-comparison-operators.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageTaskReportProperties.ObjectsTargetedCount
member.summary: Total number of objects that meet the condition as defined in the storage task assignment execution context. Filter options such as objectsTargetedCount gt 50 and other comparison operators can be used as described for Numerical properties in https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/storageservices/querying-tables-and-entities#supported-comparison-operators.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageTaskReportProperties.ObjectsOperatedOnCount
member.summary: Total number of objects that meet the storage tasks condition and were operated upon. Filter options such as objectsOperatedOnCount ge 100 and other comparison operators can be used as described for Numerical properties in https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/storageservices/querying-tables-and-entities#supported-comparison-operators.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageTaskReportProperties.ObjectFailedCount
member.summary: Total number of objects where task operation failed when was attempted. Filter options such as objectFailedCount eq 0 and other comparison operators can be used as described for Numerical properties in https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/storageservices/querying-tables-and-entities#supported-comparison-operators.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageTaskReportProperties.ObjectsSucceededCount
member.summary: Total number of objects where task operation succeeded when was attempted.Filter options such as objectsSucceededCount gt 150 and other comparison operators can be used as described for Numerical properties in https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/storageservices/querying-tables-and-entities#supported-comparison-operators.

member.summary: An update history of the ImmutabilityPolicy of a blob container.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.UpdateHistoryEntry.UpdateType
member.summary: The ImmutabilityPolicy update type of a blob container, possible values include: put, lock and extend.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.UpdateHistoryEntry.ImmutabilityPeriodSinceCreationInDays
member.summary: The immutability period for the blobs in the container since the policy creation, in days.

member.summary: Returns the date and time the ImmutabilityPolicy was updated.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.UpdateHistoryEntry.ObjectIdentifier
member.summary: Returns the Object ID of the user who updated the ImmutabilityPolicy.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.UpdateHistoryEntry.TenantId
member.summary: Returns the Tenant ID that issued the token for the user who updated the ImmutabilityPolicy.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.UpdateHistoryEntry.Upn
member.summary: Returns the User Principal Name of the user who updated the ImmutabilityPolicy.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.UpdateHistoryEntry.AllowProtectedAppendWrites
member.summary: This property can only be changed for unlocked time-based retention policies. When enabled, new blocks can be written to an append blob while maintaining immutability protection and compliance. Only new blocks can be added and any existing blocks cannot be modified or deleted. This property cannot be changed with ExtendImmutabilityPolicy API.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.UpdateHistoryEntry.AllowProtectedAppendWritesAll
member.summary: This property can only be changed for unlocked time-based retention policies. When enabled, new blocks can be written to both 'Append and Bock Blobs' while maintaining immutability protection and compliance. Only new blocks can be added and any existing blocks cannot be modified or deleted. This property cannot be changed with ExtendImmutabilityPolicy API. The 'allowProtectedAppendWrites' and 'allowProtectedAppendWritesAll' properties are mutually exclusive.

member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount
member.summary: StorageAccount.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.Name
member.summary: The name of the storage account within the specified resource group. Storage account names must be between 3 and 24 characters in length and use numbers and lower-case letters only.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.Kind
member.summary: Required. Indicates the type of storage account.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.Location
member.summary: Required. Gets or sets the location of the resource. This will be one of the supported and registered Azure Geo Regions (e.g. West US, East US, Southeast Asia, etc.). The geo region of a resource cannot be changed once it is created, but if an identical geo region is specified on update, the request will succeed.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.Sku
member.summary: Required. Gets or sets the SKU name.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.AccessTier
member.summary: Required for storage accounts where kind = BlobStorage. The access tier is used for billing. The 'Premium' access tier is the default value for premium block blobs storage account type and it cannot be changed for the premium block blobs storage account type.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.AllowBlobPublicAccess
member.summary: Allow or disallow public access to all blobs or containers in the storage account. The default interpretation is false for this property.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.AllowCrossTenantReplication
member.summary: Allow or disallow cross AAD tenant object replication. Set this property to true for new or existing accounts only if object replication policies will involve storage accounts in different AAD tenants. The default interpretation is false for new accounts to follow best security practices by default.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.AllowedCopyScope
member.summary: Restrict copy to and from Storage Accounts within an AAD tenant or with Private Links to the same VNet.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.AllowSharedKeyAccess
member.summary: Indicates whether the storage account permits requests to be authorized with the account access key via Shared Key. If false, then all requests, including shared access signatures, must be authorized with Azure Active Directory (Azure AD). The default value is null, which is equivalent to true.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.AzureFilesIdentityBasedAuthentication
member.summary: Provides the identity based authentication settings for Azure Files.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.CustomDomain
member.summary: User domain assigned to the storage account. Name is the CNAME source. Only one custom domain is supported per storage account at this time. To clear the existing custom domain, use an empty string for the custom domain name property.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.DnsEndpointType
member.summary: Allows you to specify the type of endpoint. Set this to AzureDNSZone to create a large number of accounts in a single subscription, which creates accounts in an Azure DNS Zone and the endpoint URL will have an alphanumeric DNS Zone identifier.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.EnableHttpsTrafficOnly
member.summary: Allows https traffic only to storage service if sets to true. The default value is true since API version 2019-04-01.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.Encryption
member.summary: Encryption settings to be used for server-side encryption for the storage account.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.ExtendedLocation
member.summary: Optional. Set the extended location of the resource. If not set, the storage account will be created in Azure main region. Otherwise it will be created in the specified extended location.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.Identity
member.summary: The identity of the resource.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.ImmutableStorageWithVersioning
member.summary: The property is immutable and can only be set to true at the account creation time. When set to true, it enables object level immutability for all the new containers in the account by default.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.IsDefaultToOAuthAuthentication
member.summary: A boolean flag which indicates whether the default authentication is OAuth or not. The default interpretation is false for this property.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.IsExtendedGroupEnabled
member.summary: Enables extended group support with local users feature, if set to true.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.IsHnsEnabled
member.summary: Account HierarchicalNamespace enabled if sets to true.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.IsLocalUserEnabled
member.summary: Enables local users feature, if set to true.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.IsNfsV3Enabled
member.summary: NFS 3.0 protocol support enabled if set to true.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.IsSftpEnabled
member.summary: Enables Secure File Transfer Protocol, if set to true.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.KeyExpirationPeriodInDays
member.summary: The key expiration period in days.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.LargeFileSharesState
member.summary: Allow large file shares if sets to Enabled. It cannot be disabled once it is enabled.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.MinimumTlsVersion
member.summary: Set the minimum TLS version to be permitted on requests to storage. The default interpretation is TLS 1.0 for this property.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.NetworkRuleSet
member.summary: Network rule set.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.PublicNetworkAccess
member.summary: Allow, disallow, or let Network Security Perimeter configuration to evaluate public network access to Storage Account. Value is optional but if passed in, must be 'Enabled', 'Disabled' or 'SecuredByPerimeter'.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.RoutingPreference
member.summary: Maintains information about the network routing choice opted by the user for data transfer.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.SasPolicy
member.summary: SasPolicy assigned to the storage account.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Storage.StorageAccount.Tags
member.summary: Gets or sets a list of key value pairs that describe the resource. These tags can be used for viewing and grouping this resource (across resource groups). A maximum of 15 tags can be provided for a resource. Each tag must have a key with a length no greater than 128 characters and a value with a length no greater than 256 characters.

member.summary: Gets the timestamp of the most recent instance of a failover to the secondary location. Only the most recent timestamp is retained. This element is not returned if there has never been a failover instance. Only available if the accountType is Standard_GRS or Standard_RAGRS.

member.summary: Gets the location of the geo-replicated secondary for the storage account. Only available if the accountType is Standard_GRS or Standard_RAGRS.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

Used for diagnostic scope and exception creation. This is expected to be the instance created during the construction of your main client.

The initial value of . Usually, long-running operation objects can be instantiated in two ways:
When calling a client's "Start<OperationName>" method, a service call is made to start the operation, and an instance is returned. In this case, the response received from this service call can be passed here.
When a user instantiates an directly using a public constructor, there's no previous service call. In this case, passing null is expected.
The type name of the long-running operation making use of this class. Used when creating diagnostic scopes. If left null, the type name will be inferred based on the parameter .
The attributes to use during diagnostic scope creation.
The delay strategy to use. Default is .

member.name: M:Azure.Core.OperationInternalBase.WaitForCompletionResponseAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Periodically calls until the long-running operation completes. After each service call, a retry-after header may be returned to communicate that there is no reason to poll for status change until the specified time has passed. The maximum of the retry after value and the fallback strategy is then used as the wait interval. Headers supported are: "Retry-After", "retry-after-ms", and "x-ms-retry-after-ms", Usage example:
public async ValueTask<Response<T>> WaitForCompletionAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken) => await _operationInternal.WaitForCompletionAsync(cancellationToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
member.param: A controlling the request lifetime.
member.returns: The last HTTP response received from the server, including the final result of the long-running operation.
member.exception: Thrown if there's been any issues during the connection, or if the operation has completed with failures.

member.name: M:Azure.Core.OperationInternalBase.WaitForCompletionResponseAsync(System.TimeSpan,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Periodically calls until the long-running operation completes. The interval between calls is defined by the parameter , but it can change based on information returned from the server. After each service call, a retry-after header may be returned to communicate that there is no reason to poll for status change until the specified time has passed. In this case, the maximum value between the parameter and the retry-after header is chosen as the wait interval. Headers supported are: "Retry-After", "retry-after-ms", and "x-ms-retry-after-ms". Usage example:

member.name: M:Azure.Core.OperationInternal`1.#ctor(Azure.Core.IOperation{`0},Azure.Core.Pipeline.ClientDiagnostics,Azure.Response,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair{System.String,System.String}},Azure.Core.DelayStrategy)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The long-running operation making use of this class. Passing "this" is expected.
Used for diagnostic scope and exception creation. This is expected to be the instance created during the construction of your main client.

The initial value of . Usually, long-running operation objects can be instantiated in two ways:
When calling a client's "Start<OperationName>" method, a service call is made to start the operation, and an instance is returned. In this case, the response received from this service call can be passed here.
When a user instantiates an directly using a public constructor, there's no previous service call. In this case, passing null is expected.
The type name of the long-running operation making use of this class. Used when creating diagnostic scopes. If left null, the type name will be inferred based on the parameter .
The attributes to use during diagnostic scope creation.
The delay strategy when Retry-After header is not present. When it is present, the longer of the two delays will be used. Default is .

member.summary: Periodically calls the server till the long-running operation completes.

member.returns: The last HTTP response received from the server.
member.remarks: This method will periodically call UpdateStatusAsync till HasCompleted is true, then return the final result of the operation.

member.name: P:Azure.Core.ForwardsClientCallsAttribute.SkipChecks
member.summary: Gets whether or not we should validate DiagnosticScope for this API. In the case where there is an internal API that makes the Azure API call and a public API that uses it we need ForwardsClientCalls. If the public API will cache the results then the diagnostic scope will not always be created because an Azure API is not always called. In this case we need to turn off this validation for this API only.
